# Copilot Instructions

See the short agent guidance first: [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)

Purpose
- Quick, repo-specific rules for AI coding assistants and Copilot-like agents.

Quick-edit rules (follow these when making changes)

- Read `AGENTS.md` before making edits — it contains architecture notes and run commands.
- Configuration: prefer changing `BACKENDS` via environment (`.env` / `BACKENDS` JSON). Do not hardcode model names, ports, or routes in code.
- Use the settings singleton: call `get_settings()` (in `services/gateway/src/polyglot_gateway/core/settings.py`) rather than instantiating `Settings()` directly.
- Timeouts: if you change `request_timeout_seconds` in settings, update `task_soft_time_limit`/`task_time_limit` in `services/gateway/src/polyglot_gateway/worker/celery_app.py` to match.
- Redis contract: do not rename or change Redis key prefixes `trans:` and `jobtrack:` — they are runtime contracts used by other services and tests.
- Celery task name: keep the task name `polyglot.translate` unchanged to avoid breaking running queues.
- ONNX artifacts: generated by `make export` and are git-ignored; do not check them into source control.
- Tests: run `pytest` from `services/gateway/`; tests mock `BACKENDS` and Redis (see `services/gateway/tests/conftest.py`).
- Adding backends: implement an adapter under `services/gateway/src/polyglot_gateway/worker/backends/` and add a case in `backends/registry.py` (see `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md`).
- Formatting & linting: use `ruff format .` then `ruff check .` and `pylint` per repo instructions.

If unsure, link rather than copy: prefer adding a link to `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, or the specific source file rather than duplicating detailed instructions here.

If you want, I can add repository-specific one-line code style rules or commit message guidance — tell me which to include.
